title: Floral Crunchy Brazilian Shrimp Dumplings
description: A comforting, light shrimp filling infused with hibiscus and lime, pan-seared in low-carb wonton wrappers for a soothing sick-day meal.

ingredients.name: Keto Wonton Wrappers
ingredients.notes: made from almond flour or thin radish slices

ingredients.name: Dried Hibiscus Flowers
ingredients.notes: ground into a fine powder for floral notes

ingredients.name: Dendê Oil
ingredients.notes: traditional Brazilian red palm oil for sautéing

instructions: In a medium bowl, combine the chopped shrimp, diced hearts of palm, hibiscus powder, lime zest, and coconut milk.
Mix in the cilantro and a pinch of salt until the filling is well incorporated.
Place a small teaspoon of the shrimp mixture in the center of each keto-friendly wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wrapper with water and fold into a triangle, pressing firmly to seal.
Heat the dendê oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Place the dumplings in the pan and sear for 2-3 minutes until the bottoms are golden and crunchy.
Add two tablespoons of water to the pan and immediately cover with a lid to steam for 2 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue cooking until the remaining water evaporates and the bottoms crisp up again.
Ensure the internal temperature of the shrimp has reached 145°F (63°C).
Plate the dumplings and garnish with fresh edible flowers for a floral aroma.
